name: fluxterm
description: A notebook-style terminal where commands are structured, reusable, and composable — directly in VS Code.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with FluxTerm
walkthroughs.description: Learn how to use FluxTerm's block-based terminal workflow — structured, reusable, and composable.

steps.title: Open a FluxTerm File
steps.description:
Create a new `.ftx` file via the Command Palette, or open an existing one.

[Create a New FluxTerm File](command:fluxterm.newFile)

media.altText: A .ftx file highlighted in the VS Code Explorer panel

steps.title: Run Your First Command
steps.description: Type any shell command in the Command Block at the bottom of the panel and press **Enter** to execute it. Output appears inline below the block.

media.altText: A command block with 'echo Hello FluxTerm' typed, showing output below

steps.title: Change the Working Directory
steps.description: **Double-click** the path shown in a block's context bar to edit the working directory. Type a partial path and use **Tab** or **↑↓** to navigate autocomplete suggestions. Press **Enter** to commit.

media.altText: The CWD editor showing a directory autocomplete dropdown

steps.title: Re-run or Edit Any Block
steps.description: Hover over any completed Command Block to reveal the **floating toolbar**. Click the **Refresh** icon to re-run with the current command, or edit the command text in the block and press **Enter** to execute the updated version.

media.altText: A completed block with the floating action toolbar visible on hover

steps.title: Add Markdown Documentation
steps.description: Click the **More (⋯)** button on any block's toolbar and select **Add Markdown Block** to insert a Markdown block. Use it to document your command workflows inline alongside your execution history.

media.altText: A rendered markdown block showing a Setup Guide heading and bullet list

scripts.compile: pnpm run build:css && pnpm run check-types && pnpm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: pnpm run build:css && pnpm run check-types && pnpm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: pnpm run compile-tests && pnpm run compile && pnpm run lint
